mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are negative on average over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.5%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between month-over-month changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS measures the strength of the income-consumption loop; positive co-movement indicates self-reinforcing demand expansion that drives earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS and RSAFS generates a lower mean 60-day return than either series in isolation over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in INDPRO indicates that supply-side capacity utilization is rolling over, preceding broader earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative during periods of low unemployment (late-cycle proxy), or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55% in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in HOUST below 800 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for durables, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances when HOUST < 800, or the mean return during those periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between CPIAUCSL inflation momentum and the FEDFUNDS level captures the real monetary policy stance; higher inflation alongside low nominal rates depresses real yields, reducing the real cost of corporate debt and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS levels yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or periods of positive real rates exhibit higher average 20-day returns than periods of negative real rates.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean 5-day return during trigger periods is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) directly tracks the economy's productive output and underlying demand momentum; positive growth releases predict upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth are negative on average over a 3-year rolling window, or the Rank IC of GDPC1 growth falls below 0.02.
